The following is a list of test cases that cover the requirement "Send the same info again" in the given system:

1. Test case "T-0" - Verification of encryption keys
2. Test case "T-1" - Verification of security log
3. Test case "T-2" - Verification of log date and system time
4. Test case "T-3" - Verification of synchronization with GE controlled NTP
5. Test case "T-4" - Verification of switching on and off and status of breakers from other GE system
6. Test case "T-5" - Verification of sending the same info again using different methods (file and meter)
7. Test case "T-6" - Development of test cases - The supplier shall prepare test cases based on agreed requirements, i.e. agreed Specification
8. Test case "T-7" - Testing tools - The Contractor should use a tool designated by the Contracting Authority to follow the Contracting Authority's reporting of test results. The Supplier is responsible for registering in the tool its feedback and status on the Customer's reported errors and comments. The Customer currently uses ReQtest in the defect management process.
9. Test case "T-8" - Test report - The Contractor should establish a test report for the test phases for which the Contractor is responsible during the Delivery Project, as shown in Figure 1 in clause 3.2.
10. Test case "T-9" - Training for UAT - The Contractor should carry out the necessary training and submit the relevant documentation according to point 5 before the start of the Customer's UAT.
11. Test case "T-10" - Metering units - Verification of installation of new metering units - Installation of new Metering Unit - Cat 1, 1 phase, Cat 1, 3 phase, Cat 2, Cat 1, 1 phase, Cat 1, 3 phase, Cat 2, Cat 1, 1 phase, Cat 1, 3 phase, Cat 2.
12. Test case "T-11" - Help function - Verification that there is a help function in the HES user interface that helps the User to understand how to perform tasks using the functionality of the system.
13. Test case "T-12" - Recording of measurement value - Verification that the measured value recorded and stored in the HES is uncorrupted, i.e. exactly the same value as recorded by the Metering Unit. Request a timestamped measurement value from the Metering Unit and compare what is displayed on the Meter with the information coming into the HES.
14. Test case "T-13" - Correct meter setting - Verification that the Meter Reading on the Metering Unit always matches the numerical capacity of the collected Energy Value and the display, even if the Meter Readings have rolled over.
15. Test case "T-14" - Time stamp for measurement value - Verification that all metrics (PQ) are time-stamped with the Current time when received by HES. Request a timestamped measurement value from the Metering Unit and compare what is displayed on the Meter with the information coming into the HES.
16. Test case "T-15" - Mass deletion - Verification that in HES there is a function to perform mass deletion of overall structure data objects so that all linked relations are also deleted. E.g. that this is implemented via group membership.
17. Test case "T-16" - Authorization profiles for user rights - Verification that HES has authorization profiles for the following user rights: Access operating system, start and stop system, update database, change authorization profiles, etc.
18. Test case "T-17" - Create groups - Verification that groups can be created dynamically by the User in HES using different parameters, e.g. Facility ID for export of collected data or meter software upgrade where group can be by postcode or meter identity.
19. Test case "T-18" - Energy values to HES within 2h - Verification that the Energy values (15-minute values) are delivered to HES within 2 hours from registration in the Metering Unit.
20. Test case "T-19" - Legal requirements - Kamstrup shows how they fulfill: Ellag SFS 1997:857, The Ordinance (SFS 1999:716) on metering, calculation and reporting of transmitted electricity, EIFS 2016:2 - The Swedish Energy Markets Inspectorate's Regulations and General Advice on Metering, Calculation and Reporting of Transmitted Electricity, STAFS 2016:1 - Swedac's Regulations on Measuring Instruments, STAFS 2016:4 - Swedac's Regulations and general guidelines on meters for active electrical energy, STAFS 2009:8 - Swedac's Regulations and general advice on metering systems for the measurement of transmitted electricity, STAFS 2009:9 - Swedac's Regulations and general advice on periodic inspection of meters for active electrical energy, EU MID Directive 2014/32/EU, Extended type approval and certification of Category 1 and 2 meters according to method SPM 1618, test with 12kV surge voltage, DLMS/COSEM suite, EU Regulation 2016/679 of 27 April 2016 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data, and repealing Directive 95/46/EC (General Data Protection Regulation).
21. Test case "T-20" - Management of both primary and secondary registration - Cat2 - Verify that the Metering Units can handle both primary and secondary recording of Metering Values.
22. Resistant marking - Cat1 and Cat2 - Verify that the labeling of the Meter's manufacturing number is durable for the lifetime of the Meter, i.e. no removable tape or print that can be scraped off.
